rulings.Case Title: DAVID EMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 10, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof. There is more than sufficient objective evidence that plaintiff was exposed to asbestos in Hamilton products. The bulk supplier doctrine is inapplicable. Jenkins vs. T & N PLC (1996) 45 Cal. App. 4th 1224. Defendant failed to shift burden of proof on the issue of punitive damages by failing to address special interrogatory responses 25, 26 and 27. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to intentional tort and false representation claim. There is a lack of reliance on plaintiff's part. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY STEVENS VS. YOLANDA PAUL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike First, Second, Third And Fourth Causes Of Action As A Meritless Slapp Pursuant To Ccp 425.16;Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 10, 2007, LINE 11. DEFENDANT YOLANDA PAUL'S Special Motion To Strike First, Second, Third And Fourth Causes Of Action As A Meritless Slapp Pursuant To CCP 425.16 is granted. Defendant established that the complaint alleges conduct governed by Anti-SLAPP. Plaintiff has failed to establish that plaintiff can prevail on any of the alleged causes of action. The conduct alleged is protected by the litigation privilige and/or was decided in favor of defendant and against plaintiff in case no. 619215. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY STEVENS VS. YOLANDA PAUL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 10, 2007, LINE 11. PLAINTIFF HENRY STEVENS' Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is denied based on the decision in Simmons vs. Allstate Ins. Co. (2001) 92 Cal. App. 4th 1068. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: METROPOLIS PARTNERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. 365 MAIN INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 10, 2007, LINE 12. DEFENDANTS 365 MAIN INCORPORATED and CHRISTOPHER DOLAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part; 20 days leave to amend. Overruled as to 1st and 2nd causes of action. Plaintiff alleges a breach of the exclusivity provision and damages as a consequence. A demurrer may not be used to challenge plaintiff's stated amount of damages. Sustained as to 3rd and 4th causes of action to allege the amount of damages sustained by plaintiff. Dolan my be held liable for tortious conduct. =(302/PJM/xSN)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM GWIRE VS. THE ROULAC GROUP
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 10, 2007, LINE 14. RESPONDENT THE ROULAC GROUP'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is denied. Defendant failed to file a timely cost memorandum, that is within 15 days after mailing of notice of entry of judgment. California Rule of Court 3.1700 (a)(1). In addition, the attorney's fees provision is in an agreement to indemnify and there has been no determination that plaintiff has an obligation to indemnify defendant. =(302/PJM/PB)
